title: Market Brief — Friday, March 27, 2026
summary: The LLMflation Index at 21.41 tells the whole story: inference pricing has collapsed nearly 80% from the GPT-4 baseline in just three years, and the deflationary pressure is accelerating at the bottom of the stack. DeepSeek V3.2 is now printing output tokens at $0.38/M — literally one six-hundredth of what OpenAI charged at GPT-4 launch. Meanwhile GPU spot markets remain supply-constrained across all three tiers, with availability running 18-20%. This is a market where the commodity input is tight but the finished product is in freefall. Someone is eating massive losses.
paragraphs: The LLMflation Index at 21.41 tells the whole story: inference pricing has collapsed nearly 80% from the GPT-4 baseline in just three years, and the deflationary pressure is accelerating at the bottom of the stack. DeepSeek V3.2 is now printing output tokens at $0.38/M — literally one six-hundredth of what OpenAI charged at GPT-4 launch. Meanwhile GPU spot markets remain supply-constrained across all three tiers, with availability running 18-20%. This is a market where the commodity input is tight but the finished product is in freefall. Someone is eating massive losses.

The GPU divergence overnight is worth flagging. H100 spot dropped 4.3% to $1.47/hr while H200 moved the opposite direction, up 5.3% to $2.45/hr. That H200 bid suggests workloads are migrating to newer silicon for reasoning-heavy models like o3 and R1, while H100 demand softens at the margin. B200 at $7.81/hr median with only 20% availability tells us the next-gen capacity buildout is still woefully behind demand — and anyone running frontier inference on B200s at that rate needs ultra-premium pricing to break even.

The inference margin stack is brutal and getting worse. Running Llama 70B on B200 costs $5.71/M tokens, which means every model priced below $6/M output is underwater on next-gen hardware. That catches Gemini 2.5 Flash at $2.50, DeepSeek R1 at $2.50, GPT-5 Mini at $2.00, Llama 4 Maverick at $0.60, Grok 4.1 Fast at $0.50, and DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38. Half the market is selling below production cost. Meta and DeepSeek are running deliberate subsidy strategies to grab distribution; the question is how long Google can justify Flash pricing that bleeds on its own hardware.

OpenAI is playing both ends. o3 Pro at $80/M output is a 97% margin product on H100 — that is the cash cow funding everything else. GPT-5.4 Mini at $4.50/M output barely clears the H100 cost basis at $4.29/M, running a thin 5% margin at best. The spread between OpenAI's premium reasoning tier and its commodity tier is now 177x on output pricing. That is not a product line, that is two completely different businesses duct-taped together.

Bottom line: GPU supply remains structurally tight at sub-25% availability across all tiers, which puts a floor under infrastructure value even as token pricing implodes. We would be overweight GPU compute exposure and underweight any pure-play inference provider without a captive hardware advantage. The subsidy war at the bottom of the token stack will claim casualties — likely smaller API resellers running on rented H100s at negative margins. The money is in owning the scarce input, not selling the commoditizing output.
bullets: LLMflation Index at 21.41, down roughly 79% from base 100 at GPT-4 launch, with DeepSeek component at 0.38 representing the deflationary floor.
GPU availability constrained across all tiers: H100 at 20% (49/250), H200 at 18% (63/350), B200 at 20% (58/295) — none approaching the 25% threshold for relief.
H200 spot up 5.3% day-over-day to $2.45/hr while H100 fell 4.3% to $1.47/hr, signaling a workload migration toward newer silicon for reasoning inference.
B200 spot median $7.81/hr with a wide $3.13-$9.00 range indicates a fragmented and illiquid market still in price discovery.
At least six models are priced below the $5.71/M B200 inference cost for Llama 70B, confirming widespread below-cost selling by DeepSeek, Meta, Google, and xAI.
OpenAI o3 Pro output pricing at $80/M is 211x more expensive than DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M — the widest spread in the tracked universe and a measure of how bifurcated the market has become.

Medium-tier inference margins are negative across all GPU classes: minus 43% on H100, minus 57% on H200, minus 90% on B200, making volume-tier API serving a money-losing proposition without massive scale or subsidy.
Anthropic Claude Opus 4.6 at $25/M output is the most expensive non-reasoning model in the dataset, priced at a 67% discount to o3 Pro but still commanding premium margins above 90% on H100.
